what_it_does: Defines a React canvas for browsing ConceptV component patterns, previewing their visual states, filtering by component group, generating a new variant through window.claude, previewing the generated JSON-backed HTML, and adopting it into the session-generated component list.
observations: The file defines built-in component groups for Buttons, Inputs, Cards, Chips, and Panels with ids, names, and descriptive copy.
The main canvas tracks generator panel state, prompt text, generation progress, proposed generated component data, and selected group filtering.
The generator prompt asks an AI completion API to return compact JSON containing name, group, desc, html, and hover/disabled state HTML.
Generated component previews render raw HTML strings through dangerouslySetInnerHTML, while built-in previews render React elements through Renderer.

The header copy mentions default, hover, active, and disabled states, but generated previews show default, hover, and disabled, and built-in variants use per-component state names such as focus, off, on, image, video, pending, success, and error.
The STATES constant is declared but not used in this file.
The file attaches Components and COMPONENT_GROUPS to window.
standouts: A single file combines a component gallery, state-preview matrix, group filter, and AI-assisted variant-generation workflow.
The generated-component contract is embedded directly in the prompt and relies on inline-styled HTML using ConceptV token names.
Renderer encodes many design-system examples as direct React branches keyed by component id and preview state.
conventions: Uses global React destructuring and assigns the component to window rather than using module exports.
Uses CSS custom properties for color, radius, typography, shadow, and surface tokens throughout inline style objects.
Uses compact id-keyed conditional rendering for built-in component previews instead of separate component files.
Uses toast notifications through optional window.dsaToast calls for generation and adoption feedback.
Generated previews are represented as HTML strings, while built-in previews are represented as React elements.
concerns: dangerouslySetInnerHTML renders generated HTML strings without a sanitizer in this file.
The AI response parser accepts the first JSON-looking object substring, which may hide malformed or unexpected output.
The visible canvas subtitle says active states are previewed, but this file does not render a general active state preview.
Renderer is a long id/state switch-like function, so adding built-in variants requires editing one dense function.
notes: This file is the Components canvas: a token-styled design-system gallery with built-in state previews and a session-scoped AI variant generator.
questions: Should generated HTML be sanitized or restricted before rendering?
Should active be represented consistently, removed from the subtitle, or wired into previews?
Should generated variants persist beyond the current session or remain local to the parent state?
Should built-in component renderers eventually be split by group or component id?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it is a gallery of UI patterns with live state previews and Vi generation, with the caveat that active is named in the subtitle but not generally rendered.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make ConceptV component substrates visible, comparable by state, and extendable through a constrained AI-generated JSON spec that uses the existing token system.
novelty: The distinct element is the in-canvas AI generation loop that asks for token-compatible inline HTML, previews it in the same state grid, and lets the user adopt it into the component catalogue for the session.
contribution: Supplies a token-aware component-pattern catalogue with state preview renderers, group filtering, and session-generated variant intake that a fused system could retain as an inspectable component substrate.
summary_for_embedding: React canvas that displays ConceptV component groups, built-in state previews, generated HTML previews, and a Vi-assisted generator/adoption flow for session-created component variants.
intention_for_embedding: This was for browsing and extending a ConceptV design-system component ledger while keeping generated additions constrained to the existing token language.

symbols.does: Renders the Components canvas, including header actions, generation panel, group filters, and component galleries.
symbols.description: This is the file's main React component. It owns session UI state for generation, proposal preview, selected group filtering, and total-count display, then delegates cards and state previews to smaller renderers.

symbols.does: Builds an AI prompt from the current variant request, calls window.claude.complete, parses the JSON response, and stores a proposal.
symbols.description: The function constrains the AI output to a single component variant with name, group, desc, default HTML, and hover/disabled HTML. It handles empty prompts, loading state, parse fallback through a JSON-looking substring, parse failure toasts, completion failure toasts, and final loading cleanup.

symbols.does: Adds the current proposal to generated components and closes the generation flow.
symbols.description: This function guards against a missing proposal, appends an id based on Date.now, shows a toast, clears the proposal, and closes the generator panel. It relies on the parent-provided addGenerated callback for storage.

symbols.does: Displays one component's name, id, description, generated marker, and state previews.
symbols.description: This card component provides the repeated gallery row structure. It chooses ComponentStates for generated HTML-backed components and BuiltinStates for built-in id-backed components.

symbols.does: Renders default, hover, and disabled preview cells for an HTML-backed generated component.
symbols.description: This component lays out a responsive grid with optional gold highlight styling. It passes the provided HTML strings to StateCell, falling back from missing hover or disabled state HTML to the default HTML.

symbols.does: Renders one labeled generated-component preview cell using raw HTML injection.
symbols.description: The cell centers the injected HTML, applies optional opacity, and labels the state in small uppercase text. Its key behavior is rendering the html prop with dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

symbols.does: Maps a built-in component id to a set of preview state names and renders a state grid.
symbols.description: This component contains the state list registry for each built-in component id. For each state, it creates a cell and delegates the actual visual example to Renderer.

symbols.does: Returns the concrete React preview element for a built-in component id and state.
symbols.description: Renderer is the built-in visual catalogue for buttons, inputs, cards, chips, badges, and panels. It uses id-specific conditional branches and inline token-based styles to show different visual states such as hover, focus, disabled, on/off, media type, status, and panel variants.
